title: Australian Macaroni Bolognese
description: A classic Australian pasta dish featuring ground beef, tomatoes, and herbs. Serve with a side of garlic bread for a comforting meal.
instructions: Cook the macaroni in boiling, salted water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining.
In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat. Add the chopped onion, carrots, and celery and cook until the vegetables are tender.
Add the ground beef to the pot, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks. Cook until the beef is browned, then add the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add the crushed tomatoes, beef stock, and reserved pasta water to the pot. Stir to combine, then bring the sauce to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the sauce simmer for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the cooked macaroni to the sauce and toss to combine. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the macaroni hot, topped with chopped fresh basil.
Enjoy with a side of garlic bread for a comforting meal.
